name: Mark Matthews
bio: Mark’s research explores the relationship between technology and wellbeing, focusing on how digital systems can support people with serious mental illness and how technology design influences key mental health factors such as sleep–wake cycles and circadian rhythms. His work integrates advanced behavioural sensing, interaction design, and machine learning to address complex challenges in mental health.

name: Dara Adedeji
bio: Dara is a third-year PhD candidate investigating how passive smartphone sensing can be used for real-time mood monitoring. Before joining the lab she worked as a clinical psychologist, and her research is deeply informed by patient perspectives.
